rag_rules: When repairing AGREE diagnostics, ensure that feature names like 'dps' are correctly scoped to either component type or implementation as appropriate.
Resolve mismatched input errors by ensuring AGREE expressions use valid syntax and visible identifiers.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always reference visible features or declared helpers from the same annex scope.
When a validator reports type errors, trace the root cause to unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
Use only named constants or visible feature names in component type contracts for assumptions and guarantees.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must use valid AADL feature names and declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Ensure that all identifiers in AGREE clauses are either visible features or previously declared eq helpers.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to reference implementation features in component type contracts without proper variable declarations.
Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type errors; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
